title: Static Glucagon Secretion Analysis of Isolated Islets
author: Julien Ghislain
materials: Radioimmunoassay kits are used to measure glucagon levels. Several kits are available from MilliporeSigma. For the protocol please refer to the manufacturer's instruction.

steps.title: Prepare KRB stocks, glucose and L-arginine...
steps.instruction: Prepare KRB stocks, glucose and L-arginine solutions KRB stock I: weigh 27.7 g NaCl bring to 1 L with milliQ water KRB stock II: weigh 1.494 g CaCl 2 •2H 2 O and bring to 1 L with milliQ water KRB stock III: weigh 0.648 g KH 2 PO 4 and bring to 1 L with milliQ water KRB stock IV: weigh 1.415 g KCl, 1.17 g MgSO 4 •7H 2 O and 8.52 g NaHCO 3 and bring to 1 L with milliQ water 1 Molarity (M) Glucose: weigh 18.02 g and bring to 100 mL with milliQ water. Filter sterilize using a 0.2 μm filter.

steps.title: Prepare KRB solution Determine the number...
steps.instruction: Prepare KRB solution Determine the number of static conditions for the assay in order to prepare a sufficient volume of KRB. Remember that you will have two pre-incubation steps and the picking, along with extra media to wash between these steps. In a beaker combine equal volumes of the four KRB stock solutions to achieve the desired volume. Add 2.38 mg/mL HEPES powder and swirl to dissolve. Then add 1 mg/mL BSA (fatty acid free), but do not mix as the BSA will stick to the sides. Cover with plastic wrap (put holes in top) and place in the 37 °C incubator for > 01:00:00 . Adjust the solution to pH 7.35 using 1 Molarity (M) NaOH. The solution should start at ~ pH 7.2 .

5.5 mM Glucose condition and islet picking and washing Calculate the required volume of 5.5 millimolar (mM) Glucose in KRB for the two islet pre-incubations ( 1 mL /well) plus 1 mL /well and about 70 mL for the islet picking and wash steps. Add 5.5 µL of 1 Molarity (M) Glucose/ml of KRB. 1 mM Glucose condition Calculate the required volume of 1 millimolar (mM) Glucose in KRB for the static ( 1 mL /well) and add 1 µL of 1 Molarity (M) Glucose/ml of KRB needed. 1 mM Glucose plus 10 mM L-arginine condition Calculate the required volume of 1 millimolar (mM) Glucose and 10 millimolar (mM) L-arginine in KRB for the static ( 1 mL /well) and add 1 µL of 1 Molarity (M) Glucose/ml of KRB . Then add L-arginine in powder form ( 1.75 mg/mL ) and adjust to pH 7.35 with HCl. Often there are other reagents to be added to the final static conditions, such as fatty acids, inhibitors or agonists. These additional components may require that separate KRB solutions be prepared. In the case of fatty acids addition prepare the KRB solution without BSA.

steps.title: Prepare plates Prepare islet picking plates...
steps.instruction: Prepare plates Prepare islet picking plates by adding 1 mL of 5.5 millimolar (mM) Glucose in KRB to three wells of a 24-well plate for each static sample. Prepare pre-incubation plates by adding 1 mL of 5.5 millimolar (mM) Glucose in KRB to three wells of a 24-well plate for each static sample. Repeat this step for a second pre-incubation plate. Place these plates in an incubator with 5 % volume CO2 at 37 °C . Prepare static incubation plate by adding 1 mL of experimental KRB ( 1 millimolar (mM) glucose alone or 1 millimolar (mM) glucose plus 10 millimolar (mM) L-arginine) to three wells of a 24-well plate for each static sample. Place these plates in the incubator with 5 % volume CO2 at 37 °C .

steps.title: Following isolation, the islets should be...
steps.instruction: Following isolation, the islets should be allowed to recover in recovery medium (RPMI / 10% serum / 11.1 millimolar (mM) glucose) for 01:00:00 at 37 °C . Wash the islets in a petri dish containing 20 mL of 5.5 millimolar (mM) Glucose in KRB. Pick the islets (in triplicate batches of 10) into the islet picking plate wells. Using a pipette transfer the islets from the picking plate to the first pre-incubation plate. Incubate at 37 °C for 00:20:00 . Then transfer the islets to the second pre-incubation plate. Incubate at 37 °C for 00:20:00 . Then transfer the islets to the incubation plate and incubate at 37 °C for 01:00:00 . During the incubation, label two 1.5 mL tubes per sample for collection of the KRB media containing secreted glucagon. Prepare and label 1.5 mL tubes filled with 1 mL acidified ethanol ( 75 % (v/v) ethanol / 1.5 % (v/v) HCl) for glucagon content analysis. At the end of the static incubation, collect the islets and transfer them to the tubes pre-filled with acidified ethanol. Cap the vials and store at -20 °C overnight. Then, transfer the media from each well of the static incubation into a 1.5 mL tube, centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes , transfer the supernatant to a new 1.5 mL tube and store at -20 °C until ready to complete the glucagon assay. The next day retrieve the glucagon content analysis tubes, vortex and centrifuge at 4000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Transfer the supernatant to labelled 1.5 mL tubes. Store the glucagon content samples at -20 °C , until ready to complete the glucagon assay.
